title: Candidate pairwise interaction scalable replay

queries: candidate conditioned pairwise scoring replay scalable autocomplete multiclass graph llm
candidate aware multiclass scorer pairwise interaction scalable ranking interface relbench

evidence.why_now: EXP126 showed that even after removing the training-time OOM path, the top-k dual autoregressive reranker still delivers unacceptable throughput while staying below the strict item-incoterms baseline. EXP093 already established that cheap candidate-conditioned pairwise interaction carries real salt-side signal, and EXP099 showed that the stronger autoregressive hybrid can help but at a scalability cost. The next justified step is therefore a clean replay of the cheaper pairwise-only interface under the new scalability and throughput constraints.

hypothesis: If the robust part of the autocomplete gain comes from candidate-conditioned pairwise interaction rather than from expensive autoregressive token reranking, then replaying the pairwise-only scorer should recover a meaningful fraction of the salt-side signal while restoring practical throughput and preserving a cleaner Stage 4 pretrain-compatible candidate-scoring story.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This is a replay-control style return to the cheaper candidate-conditioned pairwise scorer after the top-k autoregressive reranker line proved too slow to justify continued screening.
Use the new serial_batch_equivalent launch mode so the active representative task can absorb the idle GPU pool without silently changing effective global batch.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.

Resolved serial batch-equivalent placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPUs 5,6 with per-rank batch 2; user-ltv -> lab25211 GPUs 5,6 with per-rank batch 1; item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPUs 5,6 with per-rank batch 2.

First orchestrator status check after launch showed the serial controller active and the first representative-task window up; later tasks remained queued behind the serial controller as expected for launch_mode=serial_batch_equivalent.
Completed on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn improved strongly, user-ltv landed in the noise band, and item-incoterms regressed far below baseline even under the replay-aware effective delta.
Conclusion: pure pairwise candidate-conditioned interaction restores practical throughput but loses too much salt-side multiclass ranking signal to remain the active endpoint.
